title: The Passport (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 1971 (04.12.1971)
text: REGISTERED No. D. 221 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sab-section (I) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 220] NEW DELHI, SATURDAY, DECEMBER 4, 1971/AGRAHAYANA 13, 1893 Separate paging is given to this Part in order that it may be filed as a separate compilation MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 4th December 1971 G.S.R.1848.—In pursuance of clause (a) of section 19 of the Passports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby declares that a foreign country, namely, Pakistan, is committing external aggression against India. [No. VI/401/34/71.] ( 1259 ) -- 1 of 2 -- I260 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PART II— SEC. 3(1)] G.S.It. 1849.—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 24 of the Passports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further te amend tht Passports Rules, iyt)7, namely:— 1. ihort title aad c«wu«ama#]tt.—(l) These rules mar be called the Passport (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 1871. (2) They shall come Into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Passport Rules, 1M7,— (1) after rule 14, the following; rule shall be Inserted, namely:— "IS. Authority and form for special endorsement under section 19.—(1) Tke authority, for the purposes of section 19, shall be the passport authority. (2) Every special endorsement referred to in section IS shall be In tke following form, namely:— FORM This passport is hereby made valid under section IS of the Passports Act, 1967, for travel through or visiting Pakistan for a maximum period of days/months, from the date of this endorsement". No. VI/401/53/71. P. N. MENON, Secy. PRINTED BY THE GENERAL MANAGER, GOVERNMENT OF INDIA PRESS, MINTO ROAD, NEW EF.LHI AND PUBLISHED BY THE MANAGER OF PUBLICATIONS, DELHI, 1971 -- 2 of 2 --
